title: Express Middleware Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of application-level vs route-level usage, built-in utilities (express.json/static), and third-party tools like Morgan or Helmet.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Middleware' in an Express.js application?
options: A function that has access to the request (req) and response (res) objects, allowing you to run code or modify data before it reaches the final route handler.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which and/or what was the primarily method used to register 'Application-Level' middleware that runs for every request?
Which built-in Express middleware is commonly used to parse incoming JSON data in the request body?
Which and/or what function must be called inside a middleware to pass control to the 'next' middleware in the stack?
Does standard middleware like 'express.static()' allowing you to easily serve images, CSS, and other static files without writing custom route handlers?
options: Yes, as it is a built-in Express utility specifically for serving a directory of files.
No, it only works for HTML.
